panels.title: US Market
panels.subtitle: Your large/mid/small-cap US index exposure — classic valuation, complacency and macro bubble measures.

indicators.name: Shiller CAPE ratio
indicators.explainer: Price ÷ 10-year average inflation-adjusted earnings. The best long-run return predictor there is; readings above ~30 have historically preceded weak decade-ahead returns.

indicators.name: Buffett Indicator (Z.1 basis)
indicators.explainer: All US corporate equities (Fed Z.1, includes unlisted companies) ÷ GDP, so it prints higher than the famous Wilshire-based 150–200% quote — compare the percentile, not the headline number.

indicators.name: Tobin's Q
indicators.explainer: Market value of nonfinancial companies ÷ replacement cost of their assets. Long-run mean is ~0.75; above 1 the market prices companies above their tangible worth.

indicators.context: +0.37pp spread — un-inverted ~23 months ago; recessions historically strike 6–24 months AFTER un-inversion
indicators.name: Yield curve (10y−2y)
indicators.explainer: 10-year minus 2-year Treasury yield. Inversion is the most reliable recession lead indicator — and the hit usually comes after the curve un-inverts, not during the inversion.

indicators.name: S&P 500 ÷ M2
indicators.explainer: The index divided by the money supply — strips monetary inflation out of 'record highs'. High percentile = stocks rich even after adjusting for all the new money.

indicators.name: VIX (volatility)
indicators.explainer: The 'fear index'. A very LOW VIX means investors are complacent — a classic late-bubble tell. Spikes mean fear has arrived.

indicators.context: 2.71pp over Treasuries — spreads extremely tight — lenders pricing in near-zero risk
indicators.name: High-yield credit spread
indicators.explainer: Extra yield junk bonds pay over Treasuries. Very tight = investors pricing in almost no risk (froth); widening = stress building.

panels.title: India
panels.subtitle: Nifty large/mid/small valuation vs their own 10-year history. NSE data as of 2026-07-17.

summary.verdict: No broad froth by these measures

indicators.name: Nifty 50 P/E
indicators.explainer: Price ÷ consolidated trailing earnings for India's benchmark 50. NSE switched to consolidated EPS in 2021, so compare against the post-2021 range more than the raw long-term average.

indicators.name: Nifty 50 P/B
indicators.explainer: Price-to-book for the Nifty 50. Immune to the 2021 earnings-methodology change, so it's the cleaner long-run valuation gauge for Indian large caps.

indicators.name: Midcap 150 P/E premium
indicators.explainer: How much more expensive midcaps are than the Nifty 50. This premium blowing out is THE India froth signal — when it spiked in early 2024, SEBI forced AMCs into stress-test disclosures.

indicators.name: Smallcap 250 P/E premium
indicators.explainer: Smallcap P/E versus the Nifty 50. Small caps carry higher risk and historically trade at a discount — a fat premium means retail euphoria.

indicators.name: Nifty 50 dividend yield
indicators.explainer: Dividends ÷ price, inverted valuation: the LOWER the yield, the more expensive the market. Bottom-decile yield has marked every Indian market top.

panels.title: US Tech & Concentration
panels.subtitle: Your AAPL and tech-heavy exposure: concentration and relative valuation measures.

indicators.context: 37.61% of the index in 10 stocks — dot-com peak was ~27%
indicators.name: S&P 500 top-10 concentration
indicators.explainer: Weight of the 10 biggest stocks in the S&P 500. Your 'index' exposure is increasingly a bet on a handful of mega-cap tech names — this is how much.

indicators.name: Nasdaq 100 ÷ S&P 500
indicators.explainer: Tech's valuation premium over the broad market. At the dot-com peak this ratio collapsed 60% peak-to-trough — it is your AAPL/tech overweight's main risk.

indicators.name: AAPL trailing P/E
indicators.explainer: Apple's price ÷ trailing earnings. Apple spent 2010–2019 in the 12–20× range; the re-rating above 25× is multiple expansion, not earnings growth.

panels.title: Derivatives & Positioning
panels.subtitle: What the options market is pricing — complacency, positioning and regime signals for FnO.

summary.verdict: Mixed signals: froth building, watch closely

indicators.name: India VIX
indicators.explainer: Implied volatility of Nifty options. For an FnO trader this is the price of optionality itself: very low VIX = cheap hedges, expensive complacency, and violent unwinds when the regime flips.

indicators.name: NIFTY put/call ratio (OI)
indicators.explainer: Open-interest puts ÷ calls across all NIFTY option expiries, from the end-of-day F&O bhavcopy. Very low = everyone is long calls (euphoria); very high = crowded hedging.

indicators.name: VIX ÷ VIX3M term structure
indicators.explainer: Spot VIX versus 3-month VIX. Deep contango (<0.85) = markets paying nothing for near-term risk; a flip above 1.0 (backwardation) is the single cleanest 'regime changed' alarm for option sellers.

indicators.name: CBOE SKEW
indicators.explainer: Prices of far out-of-the-money S&P puts vs at-the-money. High SKEW with low VIX = smart money quietly paying up for crash insurance while the surface looks calm.
